metadata.title: MCP Config Generator and JSON Debugger
metadata.description: Browser-local MCP JSON config scratchpad with common mcpServers checks, security prompts, and setup boundaries for local AI developer tools.

page.title: MCP Config Generator and JSON Debugger

tool.title: Check an MCP JSON config before wiring it into your client
tool.description: Paste or adapt a local MCP config, validate JSON syntax, and review the common mcpServers shape before you add a server to Claude Code, Cursor, or another MCP-capable workspace.
tool.source_boundary: Motubrain.org is an independent utility page. It does not claim official MCP, Claude, Cursor, or server-specific authority; use it as a preflight checklist before checking the docs for the exact client and server you run.
checklist: Keep the config as valid JSON: quoted keys, no comments, no trailing commas.
Prefer explicit server names so you can audit which tool can read which files or URLs.
Review command, args, env, and remote URL fields before trusting copied snippets.
Keep tokens, passwords, and API keys out of shared screenshots and pasted configs.

workflow.title: MCP config preflight sequence
workflow.description: Use this compact pass before turning on a new local or remote MCP server.

items.title: Parse the JSON
items.description: Fix syntax first. JSON config files usually reject comments, single quotes, and trailing commas even when a blog snippet looks close.

items.title: Check server shape
items.description: Confirm the server entry has the launch method your client expects, commonly a local command with args or a remote URL.

items.title: Map access
items.description: Write down what each server can read or execute: project folders, shell commands, browser access, databases, or remote APIs.

items.title: Confirm sources
items.description: Open the package, repository, or vendor docs for every server before installing a copied command.

boundaries.title: What this MCP helper does and does not verify
boundaries.description: The page is intentionally conservative: it catches common config mistakes and points you back to primary sources.

items.title: JSON syntax and common shape
items.description: The scratchpad checks whether the file parses and whether mcpServers entries look like usable server objects.

items.title: Security review prompts
items.description: Warnings call out remote URLs, secret-like values, env fields, and file access boundaries before you paste the config into a client.

items.title: Not an official validator
items.description: Exact fields, supported transports, and server options belong to your MCP client and each server's documentation.

items.title: Local AI workspace context
items.description: If you are building a broader self-hosted AI workspace, compare server permissions with the local app boundary.

faq.title: MCP config FAQ
faq.description: Practical boundaries for MCP JSON setup and debugging.

items.question: Is this an official MCP config generator?
items.answer: No. It is an independent browser-local scratchpad for JSON syntax checks and common setup hygiene. Confirm final details in the documentation for your client and MCP server.

items.question: Does the tool upload my config?
items.answer: No. The checks run in the browser component. Avoid pasting secrets anyway, because configs are often copied into tickets, chats, screenshots, or shared repos.

items.question: Why does the page warn about remote URLs?
items.answer: A remote MCP server can change the trust boundary. Confirm TLS, authentication, logging, and what data the server can access before enabling it.

items.question: Can this verify Claude Code or Cursor-specific behavior?
items.answer: It can catch common JSON mistakes, but it does not replace client-specific docs or runtime testing. Client-specific field support may differ.
